slides.title: The && Operator ⚡
slides.content:
# Render Only If Condition is True In JavaScript, `true && expression` always evaluates to `expression`, and `false && expression` evaluates to `false`. React ignores `false`, `null`, `undefined`, and `true` when rendering, so you can use `&&` to conditionally include JSX. ```jsx {unreadMessages.length > 0 && (

You have {unreadMessages.length} unread messages.

)} ``` If the left side is false, nothing renders. If true, the right side renders.

slides.title: Common Pitfall ⚠️
slides.content:
## Don't Use && with Numbers

Be careful: `0 && <Component />` will render `0` (because 0 is a falsy value that React renders). Always use a boolean condition, like `count > 0 && ...`.

quizData.question:
What will be rendered if `items` is an empty array? ```jsx
{items.length &&

Items exist

}
```
quizData.explanation: `items.length` is 0, and 0 is rendered. Use `items.length > 0 && ...` to avoid this.
